item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've read that comment, that's honestly part of what I enjoyed about this, even if the Kindle aspect feels more like an afterthought. My criticisms aside, this article's given me a few vague ideas for a future Serpent's Hand/Library article that uses and builds upon SCP-1326.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't address most of your issues but I did find a comment from the author describing the scip as a sort of Wonderer's Library Kindle that's given to certain individuals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Despite there being a few places where the tone isn't as tightened up or polished as it could be — which is understandable, given this article's age — the details behind the design of the book and the way it functions were more than enough to keep my interest.

I wasn't a fan of where the article went from there, so I'll point out the specifics below:

+ Pros and Cons
– hide block

Things I liked: I liked the arachnid entities. They were adequately creepy without seeming forced. I also enjoyed reading about the concept that the Wanderers' Library has security on a book-by-book basis. I thought that idea added to the Library's lore in a constructive way.

Things I disliked: The Incident Report.

For one, I found it exceedingly contrived that the researchers at Site-19 would be negligent enough to not only fail to maintain the anomaly for a full month, but also, forget to close the containment cell.

Next, the incident itself felt unnecessary. I get that the author included it to tell a greater story about the Library owning this book, and that they're not too keen on the Foundation misusing it. But the fact that there's a containment breach involved feels synthetic, and nonsensical (where are the security personnel, MTF, or Foundation personnel in general?). This ultimately paints the researchers as incompetent (in the biggest, most well-protected Site in most canons, no less), which I don't enjoy.

As an aside: Why is this book even in the possession of the Foundation? If the Librarians have knowledge of it, why didn't they enact their punishment sooner? Why allow the Foundation to keep it for their own purposes — wouldn't they at least take the book back? It feels out of character.

Finally, I was not a huge fan of the writing style of that section; I thought it was a bit sloppy, and the expungements felt lazy on the part of the author and dissatisfying.

TLDR: There is a good concept in here, but the execution muddles that concept and leaves much to be desired.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was an awesome scip with a fantastic open research log. I was ready to give it an upvote right until the last entry in the incident log. The 'It ate his sooooouuuullll!!' ending really detracted from this one for me. If it had been kept subtle as it was in the research log (a complete biography including innermost thoughts) I would have +1ed in a heartbeat. As is that ending pushed me to a no vote.

item.title: Image Update
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1326's images were removed because we could not determine whether or not the images were properly licensed or attributed. They may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact Apoplexic or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

Noting the manual removal to avoid a double jarvis post.

E: In what is certainly a site record response time, I've returned the second image to the article, with the addition of attribution on request of the owner, Anji Marth. Anji also expressed enjoyment of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And this is why you don't hang out with the Serpent's Hand. The addition at the end with the tolls was freaky, very well toned, and delivers a very important message about the Library.

Remember kids, find another way to get your books.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Giant talking spiders are creepy, yes. But what creeps me out the most about SCP-1326-2 instances? The fact that they have skeletons. Can you imagine normal sized spiders with skeletons? Imagine the sound if you stepped on one or hit one with newspaper, etc. And I thought normal spiders were bad.

+1 for making my spider phobia slightly more potent, and because I love the list of works found within SCP-1326, and the whole idea of this SCP, really!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was trying to convey that line was coming from one of the 1326-2 during a collection event after the one that took the researcher.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anyone else bothered by

must be replaced ASAP

?? Just me? Oh bother.. :(

I can enjoy it, but the ASAP breaks the tone for me. Also, the incident recorded audio is odd to me. Not sure I understand what is going on there.

The Library does not accept unpaid tolls.

Return your books on time.

And the researcher was heard saying, "kill me." while he was being abducted, or the book stole his voice, or?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you let us see the first letter of each doctor's name? At first I thought it was the same guy who got absorbed into the book.

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded:
Well, let me state up front that There Is No Canon, and you can do whatever you want with this, period.

But, this doesn't really mesh with how the Library's been established. The Docents can find you anywhere you go if you ever got a Library Card, which you'd certainly need for something like this. And, of course, Docents don't and can't speak.

If you're curious, the FAQ Lycan linked above has been expanded and (hopefully) better organized here: http://wanderers-library.wikidot.com/the-universe

Let me emphasize again, though, that you are not by any means expected to follow this. I am linking it here because you've shown interest, that's all. :)

Also, on that note, you would be welcome at the Library if you care to join the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I imagine my persona might be crazy enough to try that himself, but self-inserts tend not to be very well-received.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd assume a portal to the Library.

Would probably make a good Tale.

P.S. SH tag added.

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded: I understand. In fact, I already posted my idea of how that would turn out in one of the older comments.

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded:
Delayed reaction due to me not having much access to the internet after posting what I did until now. I was in no way comparing 1326 and 1065 implying a rip-off; if anything I was pointing out that one was COLLECTING all knowledge (by violence if necessary), and one was trying to WIPE OUT collected knowledge; them being thrown into battle would be hysterical, in a protracted and boring fashion.

I did quite enjoy your article all on its own, I'm just rather silly at times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just thought of something:

What would happen if you put this thing into SCP-826? Portal to the Library or universe constructed from the literature it contains?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah. Fair enough then, I guess I can make the recovery log describe how this was recovered from the ownership of a suspected SH member so the tag will be valid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There isn't a "Library" tag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably "Library", not entirely sure about Serpent's Hand since they don't really own the place, they're just its most frequent customers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me that over in Game Day, there was a story leading up to the GOC planning a raid on The Library…what could possibly go wrong with that?
I'm still hoping Gears writes that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Added a reference to the Wanderer's Library in Document 1326-82. One of the combinations tested now yields the Book of Long Shadows, a document from the Library.

Also, how many facial features can a gatherer even have? It looks like a mix of traits between Gollum and a giant spider.

Now I see why Dr. ██████ wanted the foundation to kill him.

P.S. Should I add the "serpents-hand" tag to this SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
People get turned into gatherers? Okay, now I do like the second picture.
I'd imagine one of the gatherers shares similar facial features with Dr. ██████, not necessarily voice, but… yeah, that's creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good. Makes it more clear/creepy that rather than just being mind-wiped, and his personality fed into a book Dr. ██████ was turned into a gatherer.

My only advice is….

1. The foundation doesn't get Dr. ██████'s body back. They do get his clothes, stripped of labels. A blank foundation security card. Bloody dental fillings….

2. Add the recovery log idea. It's found in the ██████████ library following all the other books being stripped of writing. Or some guy with suspected ties to the Hand is caught by the foundation, and has it on him. Or maybe it was found in Lord Blackwood's vault as one of the things he picked up on his travels….

Loads of ideas for this thing's history anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of them jumping into the pages to leave, disappearing without a trace.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. I was going to take that further, but I had to go rafting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe your confused, sir. If two articles are similar, that implies there effect is similar. Which they aren't really. One is a radius of effect which gives you random information. This is a book that not only scans other books, but contains every price of literature in every universe it can access. So because they have to do with information, they are similar? By tha logic 173 and 682 are similar because the kill people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Modified the Incedent Report with new changes. How does it look now?

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded:
I think I like that idea, too. Might make more sense if the Library just left these in various worlds as a means of collecting information rather than making it some sort of "premium membership" bonus. Not sure how I would be able to work all that into the article itself (might be good Tale material, though), but at least I can change the incident report to incorporate the new information about 1326-2.

Recovery log might potentially help, but I'm not sure what to do for that ATM.

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded: Interesting. Now, I like that. I don't, however, like the idea that it's given to people to collect books, though that may or may not just be personal preference. Take it as you will, although I'll throw up the idea that it's not actually provided to people. What if the Library just spawned it in a random populated area and let people figure it out?

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for showing me that. Now I have some new ideas to go with some old ones I had in mind:

SCP-1326 is like the Library's version of a Kindle, given to certain individuals tasked with gathering new books and information to add to the Library in exchange for the privilege of accessing the Library from anywhere outside. New information must be gathered regularly as a sort of "service fee"; failure to pay this fee results in confiscation of information by 1326-2 (which could either be a new type of "staff" or just Pages sent to confiscate information due to their ability to fit through the SCP); information taken in this manner is permanently removed as punishment for failure to pay the fee.

Individuals that harm 1326-2 have a separate punishment: they are taken to the Library by 1326-2 (possibly handed off to Docents somewhere inside), where their thoughts and memories are removed and documented as books and what remains is converted to a new instance of 1326-2. This new instance may be expelled with the directive to collect new information as part of its punishment, or it may only become active on "overdue fine" events. Converted instances may also possibly retain some semblance of their former selves (The converted Dr. ██████ would repeatedly vocalize requests to be killed).

I was thinking a recovery log would imply its former owner failed to pay his due, and was "collected" by 1326-2 as punishment. After the Foundation found it, they technically became its new owners and thus would bear the task of providing the Library with new books and information.

…let's hope we don't run out of useless crap to feed it with.

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded:
We should really stop communicating through edits. That sounds fun, Cobalt.

Anyway, the Library does have canon. Moose and Mann have set up an FAQ, which you might want to look through.

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded:
I didn't know that was a thing. I like those Library Rules, though.

EDIT: Thanks. I think I asked for a better idea for what they would say before, and I suppose something archaic like that would work. What if I combined that with the rule about returning books on time?

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded:
Sorry, was I referencing again?

EDIT: Eh. I still don't like it. Although… when I apply thought to it, I imagine the Library communicating in an archaic way. "You have not payed tribute, the Library soes not accept unpaid tolls", that sory of thing. Bah, do what you will.

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded:
Wait, what?

The other thing: That's what we think. The Library apparently thinks otherwise.

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded:
The problem is that Docents (and by the way, to the best of my knowledge, Docents, not Pages, take people away) have no reason to say anything.

Another thing: why are the Librarians coming at all? No-one's failed to return books. They've just failed to provide them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I picture both books on opposite ends of a miniature boxing ring, surrounding crowd chanting "fight! fight! fight!" to apparently no avail. Suddenly, 1326-2 swarm out of the book and throw 1065 out of the ring, then search for a more acceptable fee. The stadium is then evacuated while security staff fend off the swarm of 1326-2 with scraps of paper with random factoids written on them.

item.title: Re: Slight mixup with Addendums and Incidents
item.content:encoded: Good point. Edited.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *announcer voice* This is truly a spectacle to behold. SCP-1326, the collection of what is speculated at all literature ever, versuses his old rival SCP-1065, the harbinger of devastation to all knowledge. Who will emerge victorious? Only time will tell.

item.title: Slight mixup with Addendums and Incidents
item.content:encoded:
I'm kind of confused.

Show Addendum

Close

Incident Report 1326-█:

Shouldn't that "Addendum" collapsible read…..

Incident Report 1326-█

Close

…..as there is another Addendum below it, the "overdue" line?

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded: Oh gosh, that makes me extremely happy for some reason.

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded:
It was either that or just disconnected exclamations of stuff like "Overdue! Overdue!", "The Library does not tolerate late returns!", and other such phrases.

Any other ideas what they should sound like, or should I just say something clinical, not directly quoting 1326-2 but mentioning that translations of vocalizations made in known languages all invariably reference confiscation of overdue materials, and most notably "The Library".

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded:
Squeee. Although that's not how I imagine the Library would communicate, since it sounds like a legal document. But still. Squeee.

References aside, I should say it's (to the best of my knowledge) poor form to use quotations unless directly citing something: otherwise it looks like a lazy attempt to easily describe your phenomenon using non-clinical terms. I was also a little disappointed when I got to that Addendum, since when I first looked this over, I assumed that skeleton was the remains of some personnel. But then, Pages are fun as well.

item.title: Re: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded:
How's this:

Addendum:Security footage of the event reveals SCP-1326-2 make vocalizations resembling human speech while active. Most of these vocalizations are incoherent,
but analysis reveals some instances are vocalizing in known languages. The following is a transcript of vocalizations made by 1326-2, translated into English if not already spoken as such:

You have failed to return your books on time. All overdue materials will be confiscated in addition to a late fee. The Library does not tolerate failure to return rented materials.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So… you want that as a standard scan or a -2 event?

item.title: Wait, the foundation has a Thunderdome Site?
item.content:encoded:
O.K. now let's compare this to other SCPs in an attempt to disprove everyone's complaints that it ripped them off.

SCP-1065 - Doesn't seem to be related at all, besides being a SCP item which is also a book. I like SCP-1065, but it really doesn't resemble this very much.

SCP-028 - A place which gives people going into it a random bit of weird information. This consists of a book which contains rather odd works of literature. Not much comparison.

SCP-887 - A guy who writes weirdly random stuff he has no way of knowing. This is a book with the ability to take other books into itself, apparently from outside anything the foundation knows about. Some comparison is valid, but not as much as everyone seems to think. They are both SCPs for example….

Also, as for the idea of edits to this, my advice would be to simply add the stuff about the spider-things talking, leave Document 1326-82 open for editing, and use any other stuff as foundation Tales.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This thing. My books.
Site 17 Thunderdome.
NOW.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with the idea that this should be linked with the Serpent's Hand in the manner I suggested in Document 1326-82, and with anqxyr that the idea of the spider-things talking would be astoundingly creepy.

Best if they use snippets of every language in the book, for example one might show up babbling about not returning books on time, in a polite tone of voice and fluent [ADD LANGUAGE HERE]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the tips, implemented. Interesting idea with the 1326-2, since I had actually planned for this to be connected with Serpent's Hand, the GoI operating from the Library…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really dislike that you refer to the book simply as SCP. This is very informal. "SCP-1326", "the object" or even "the book" are all much better alternatives.

may be replaced ASAP.

I'm not sure that "ASAP" can be used in a clinical document, and even if I wrong, "may" doesn't work with "ASAP" at all; try "must" instead.

related to SCP's under or out of Foundation custody.

There shouldn't be an apostrophe there, and what I said above about SCP still applies.

The core idea is similar to a both a very common trope and another SCP article, but I am upvoting for the spider-thingies, and the image too. The only thing that could make them better is if they would berate, in human voice, everyone they encounter for not returning their books in time.

I like the corridor re-entry method better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1: How do you do that, exactly? I know you offered to do that yourself earlier, is that offer still open?
2: I got the idea for the page flip thing from chat, hence the change. I suppose either way could work, maybe I'll call a vote to see which one people like more.
3: By the way, thanks for all your help with this article, both pre- and post-completion.

To readers: Which method of re-entry into SCP-1326 by -2 would you prefer:

-disappearing into rapidly flipping pages, or
-book opens to dark corridor the -2 swarm into

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only remaining advice is to:

1. Switch the pictures from the links to the SCPfuel page, to the links on this page.
2. Use the idea of a dark corridor extending past the edge of the book as the method via which SCP-1326-2 renter the book rather than flipping pages like you had in sandbox.

Other than this, this SCP is basically perfect. I am secure in my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely written, and the pictures work well for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good stuff. Not really a big fan of either picture, but the article is so well written around them, I really don't care.
Here, take this: +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Going back and forth on whether the second picture is cheesy or scary, but I think I can live with it either way. +1'ed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Me like.
I find the behaviour of its normal scanning method to be a bit fetched/outright magical.
But I really like the idea. Document 82 really did it for me. Though I wonder if using the word 'List' alone is best.
'list extract' maybe as it is non-exhaustive?
+1, no remorse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
benevolent termination authorized by 05█.

i would recommend you to use "euthanize", because the word "termination" is overused

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh god that second picture got me. x.x

Love the very last expungement; the note afterward about "benevolent termination" makes it work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well yours turned out a hell of a lot better than mine. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh yeah, that one. Funnily enough, when I tried to get some feedback on chat, that SCP's author was, too. Both of us were confused because everyone's feedback was book SCP related, but sometimes got directed to the wrong recipient.
